mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over two months signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM signals yield negative 60d returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production below negative 1.0 percent reflects acute supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, triggering short-term risk-off selling in equities.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 60% of triggered observations during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 5.0 percent erodes real corporate margins and forces discount rate repricing, generating negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation shocks rather than organic job growth, forecasting negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Divergent PAYEMS and UNRATE signals yield positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window or 5-day returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 levels.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, industrial production growth above 1.0 percent month-over-month signals capacity utilization peaks and impending manufacturing slowdown, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of triggered observations during late-cycle phases.
